subject: Statistics
title: The Central Limit Theorem in Action
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Central Limit Theorem in Action.

hook.text: Take any ugly, skewed dataset. Sample from it 50 times, compute the mean. Repeat. A bell curve forms.

buildup.text: The Central Limit Theorem says sample means always become normal — regardless of the original shape.

discovery.text: Exponential, uniform, bimodal — all produce bell-shaped averages when samples are large enough.

twist.text: This is why confidence intervals and hypothesis tests work. They depend on means being normal.

climax.text: Without the Central Limit Theorem, most of modern statistics simply wouldn't function.

punchline.text: Enough averages always make a bell curve.

quiz.question: What does the CLT make possible?
options: Confidence intervals and hypothesis tests by making sample means normally distributed
Exact predictions of individual data points
Eliminating the need for data collection
